evidence_quotes.quote: the protection of, and the ownership of, digital goods like Video Games, and similar types of digital media.
Terms of Service (ToS) and End-User License Agreements (EULA's) allowing them to unilaterally, and effectively, pull the plug on said content, turning it into the digital equivalent of E-Waste.

paragraphs.text: Regarding the DFA, I too like many others here, would also like to voice my desire for the DFA to include the protection of, and the ownership of, digital goods like Video Games, and similar types of digital media. As the planned obsolescence by the Gaming Industry, for goods the consumer paid for, is an inherently predatory and unfair business model as shown with their Terms of Service (ToS) and End-User License Agreements (EULA's) allowing them to unilaterally, and effectively, pull the plug on said content, turning it into the digital equivalent of E-Waste. The Stop Killing Games initiative has shown, with now about over 1 Million signatures having been collected, that there exists a large movement behind these wishes. Publishers and Companies shouldn't be able to, effectively, destroy digital content that money has been paid for by the consumer. This is why games need a clear end-of-life plan, if they are dependent on services hosted by the publisher/developer, so that the consumer can continue enjoying and using the video game/media that they have paid for.Regarding the DFA, I too like many others here, would also like to voice my desire for the DFA to include the protection of, and the ownership of, digital goods like Video Games, and similar types of digital media. As the planned obsolescence by the Gaming Industry, for goods the consumer paid for, is an inherently predatory and unfair business model as shown with their Terms of Service (ToS) and End-User License Agreements (EULA's) allowing them to unilaterally, and effectively, pull the plug on said content, turning it into the digital equivalent of E-Waste. The Stop Killing Games initiative has shown, with now about over 1 Million signatures having been collected, that there exists a large movement behind these wishes. Publishers and Companies shouldn't be able to, effectively, destroy digital content that money has been paid for by the consumer. This is why games need a clear end-of-life plan, if they are dependent on services hosted by the publisher/developer, so that the consumer can continue enjoying and using the video game/media that they have paid for.
